specs.label: Harvard General Resume
writingRules: Every core experience should keep factual evidence from the original resume.
Each major experience or project should normally contain 2-4 role-relevant bullets.
Skills must be grouped and connected to evidence, not dumped as isolated keywords.
Do not include report commentary, references, scoring, or explanations in the resume body.
Prefer concrete evidence, scope, tools, and outcomes over vague self-evaluation.
Use target-role language only when supported by candidate facts.
bannedAiExpressionRules: Avoid generic AI-sounding claims such as passionate, dynamic, results-driven, proven track record, responsible for, leveraged, utilized, various, numerous, and cutting-edge unless context makes them factual.
Avoid unsupported superlatives, inflated ownership, fake metrics, and keyword stuffing.
Do not write meta-commentary, explanation of edits, gaps, or follow-up questions inside the resume.

Each major experience or project should normally contain 2-4 role-relevant bullets.
Skills must be grouped and connected to evidence, not dumped as isolated keywords.
Do not include report commentary, references, scoring, or explanations in the resume body.
Prefer concrete evidence, scope, tools, and outcomes over vague self-evaluation.
Use target-role language only when supported by candidate facts.
bannedAiExpressionRules: Avoid generic AI-sounding claims such as passionate, dynamic, results-driven, proven track record, responsible for, leveraged, utilized, various, numerous, and cutting-edge unless context makes them factual.
Avoid unsupported superlatives, inflated ownership, fake metrics, and keyword stuffing.
Do not write meta-commentary, explanation of edits, gaps, or follow-up questions inside the resume.
